nodes.text: Good evening. The day is behind you now. This won't take long — just a few honest questions. No right answers. Only true ones.
Let's start simple. If today were a weather report, what would it be?
options: Clear skies — things went my way
Partly cloudy — mixed, but manageable
Overcast — heavy and slow
Stormy — things kept going wrong
answer=Clear skies — things went my way:A1_Q_HIGH
answer=Partly cloudy — mixed, but manageable:A1_Q_HIGH
answer=Overcast — heavy and slow:A1_Q_LOW
answer=Stormy — things kept going wrong:A1_Q_LOW

nodes.text: You described today as "{A1_OPEN.answer}". When something went well, what made it happen?
options: I prepared — I was ready for it
I adapted when things shifted
The team came through together
It just happened to work out

nodes.text: You described today as "{A1_OPEN.answer}". When things got difficult, what was your first instinct?
options: Find one thing I could still control
Wait for someone else to sort it
Push harder on my own
Feel like the situation was against me
answer=I prepared — I was ready for it:A1_Q2_INT
answer=I adapted when things shifted:A1_Q2_INT
answer=The team came through together:A1_Q2_EXT
answer=It just happened to work out:A1_Q2_EXT
answer=Find one thing I could still control:A1_Q2_INT
answer=Push harder on my own:A1_Q2_INT
answer=Wait for someone else to sort it:A1_Q2_EXT
answer=Feel like the situation was against me:A1_Q2_EXT

nodes.text: Think about one decision you made today — even a small one. What were you trying to do?
options: Solve a problem before it got worse
Protect someone else from hassle
Keep myself steady and focused
Move something forward, even slightly

nodes.text: Was there a moment today where you had more choice than you initially used?
options: Yes — I see it now, in hindsight
Maybe — I'm not sure I recognized it at the time
Not really — it was genuinely constrained
I didn't feel like I had options at all
answer=Solve a problem before it got worse:A1_R_INT_STRONG
answer=Move something forward, even slightly:A1_R_INT_STRONG
answer=Protect someone else from hassle:A1_R_INT_SOFT
answer=Keep myself steady and focused:A1_R_INT_SOFT
answer=Yes — I see it now, in hindsight:A1_R_EXT_AWARE
answer=Maybe — I'm not sure I recognized it at the time:A1_R_EXT_AWARE
answer=Not really — it was genuinely constrained:A1_R_EXT_GENTLE
answer=I didn't feel like I had options at all:A1_R_EXT_GENTLE

nodes.text: You stayed in motion today. When things shifted, you looked for what you could affect — not just what was happening to you. That's not optimism. That's a discipline.
You chose direction today, even quietly. Keeping yourself steady or protecting someone else from friction — those are acts of agency too. The steering doesn't always look dramatic.

That noticing — even in hindsight — is the start of something. You can't always catch the choice in real time. But when you see it afterward, that's the muscle strengthening. Tomorrow, the window might be a little wider.

Some days really are constrained. Not every hard thing is a hidden opportunity. But even in tight situations, there's usually one small thing within reach — even if it's just how you held yourself through it.
Alright — you've thought about how you handled today. Now let's look at what you gave.
Think about the interactions you had today. What was the general texture of how you showed up?
options: I was mostly giving — energy, help, focus
I was mostly receiving — support, guidance, answers
It was transactional — I did my part, others did theirs
I felt like I was owed more than I got
answer=I was mostly giving — energy, help, focus:A2_Q_CONT
answer=I was mostly receiving — support, guidance, answers:A2_Q_ENT
answer=It was transactional — I did my part, others did theirs:A2_Q_CONT
answer=I felt like I was owed more than I got:A2_Q_ENT

nodes.text: Was there a moment today where you went a little beyond what was asked of you?
options: Yes — I helped with something outside my lane
Yes — I taught or explained something to someone
I don't think so — I stuck to my work
I'm not sure — I didn't think about it like that

nodes.text: When you didn't get the recognition or support you expected today, how did it land?
options: It stung, honestly — I noticed the gap
I got frustrated and it affected my focus
I let it go and kept moving
I wondered what I'd done wrong
answer=Yes — I helped with something outside my lane:A2_R_CONT_HIGH
answer=Yes — I taught or explained something to someone:A2_R_CONT_HIGH
answer=I don't think so — I stuck to my work:A2_R_CONT_LOW
answer=I'm not sure — I didn't think about it like that:A2_R_CONT_LOW
answer=I let it go and kept moving:A2_R_ENT_SHIFT
answer=I wondered what I'd done wrong:A2_R_ENT_SHIFT
answer=It stung, honestly — I noticed the gap:A2_R_ENT_STUCK
answer=I got frustrated and it affected my focus:A2_R_ENT_STUCK

nodes.text: You moved toward people today — not because you had to, but because you could. That kind of discretionary effort is the invisible glue in any team. It doesn't always get named, but it gets felt.
Doing your work well is real contribution — don't understate it. But there might be one person tomorrow who'd be glad you showed up for them in a small way. Worth watching for.

You noticed the gap, and you didn't let it consume you. That's harder than it sounds. The question worth sitting with: was today more about what you gave, or what you expected to receive? No wrong answer — just honest accounting.

It's worth asking: when we feel overlooked, is it because we weren't seen — or because we were measuring ourselves against what we expected to receive? Both can be true. One of them you can do something about.
Last stretch. You've looked inward — at your agency, at what you gave. Now let's zoom out. Who else was in today's picture?
When you replay today in your head right now, who's in the frame?
options: Mostly me — my tasks, my stress, my wins
Me and a few specific people I worked with
The team as a whole — we were in it together
Someone I'm trying to help, serve, or protect
answer=Mostly me — my tasks, my stress, my wins:A3_Q_SELF
answer=Me and a few specific people I worked with:A3_Q_MID
answer=The team as a whole — we were in it together:A3_Q_WIDE
answer=Someone I'm trying to help, serve, or protect:A3_Q_WIDE

nodes.text: That's honest. When you think about the most stressful part of today — was anyone else affected by it, beyond you?
options: Yes — and they probably had it harder than I did
Yes — but I was more focused on my own stress
I don't know, I wasn't thinking about others much
It was really just my problem to carry

nodes.text: Think about one of those people. Was there a moment where you considered their experience — not just how the situation affected you?
options: Yes — I adjusted something because of how they seemed
I thought about it, but didn't change anything
Not really — I was in my own head
I genuinely didn't have the bandwidth

nodes.text: When you think about the person or group you're ultimately doing this work for — the end user, the customer, the people it touches — how present were they in how you made decisions today?
options: Very — I kept coming back to their experience
Somewhat — I thought about it at key moments
Not much — I was focused on immediate deliverables
I'm not sure who that person is, honestly
answer=Yes — and they probably had it harder than I did:A3_R_SELF_OPENING
answer=Yes — but I was more focused on my own stress:A3_R_SELF_HONEST
answer=I don't know, I wasn't thinking about others much:A3_R_SELF_HONEST
answer=It was really just my problem to carry:A3_R_SELF_HONEST
answer=Yes — I adjusted something because of how they seemed:A3_R_MID_HIGH
answer=I thought about it, but didn't change anything:A3_R_MID_LOW
answer=Not really — I was in my own head:A3_R_MID_LOW
answer=I genuinely didn't have the bandwidth:A3_R_MID_LOW
answer=Very — I kept coming back to their experience:A3_R_WIDE_HIGH
answer=Somewhat — I thought about it at key moments:A3_R_WIDE_MID
answer=Not much — I was focused on immediate deliverables:A3_R_WIDE_MID
answer=I'm not sure who that person is, honestly:A3_R_WIDE_UNCLEAR

nodes.text: You saw that someone else was in the storm too — maybe deeper than you. That noticing matters. Meaning often lives in that gap: when we stop measuring our day only by how it landed on us.

Honestly: when we're stressed, the radius of concern narrows. That's not a character flaw — it's just how attention works under pressure. The question for tomorrow: is there one person in your orbit whose experience you could make slightly easier?

You adjusted — you let someone else's experience change how you showed up. That's perspective-taking in action, not theory. Most people think about others; fewer actually shift their behavior because of it.

Some days you're just in your own head — that's real. But the fact that you noticed someone else is already a kind of orientation toward them. Tomorrow, see if you can close the gap between noticing and adjusting.

You held the end of the chain in mind today — the person on the other side of your work. That's a rare and generative thing. It turns effort into purpose. Not everyone finds that orientation; you were living in it today.

The connection to the person your work ultimately serves doesn't have to be constant — even glimpsing it occasionally changes the texture of effort. See if you can make that glimpse a little more deliberate tomorrow.
Worth pausing on: who does your work actually reach? Sometimes that connection gets buried under tasks and deadlines. Finding it — even abstractly — can change the meaning of the same exact effort.
Rest well.

axis1.external: a tendency to look outward for causes

axis3.mid: relational — focused on those close to you
axis3.self: inward — centered on your own experience
axis3.self_opening: inward, with a crack of light through it

reflections.internal_contribution_wide: You moved through today as an agent — you shaped what you could, gave more than you took, and kept a wider group in view. That's a full day.
reflections.internal_contribution_mid: You had ownership today, and you gave something to the people around you. The circle of concern is already there — see if you can nudge it outward tomorrow.
reflections.internal_contribution_self: You handled things with agency and showed up as a giver. The next layer is widening the lens — whose experience are you part of, beyond what's immediately visible?
reflections.internal_entitlement_wide: There's an interesting tension here: you saw your own role clearly, kept others in view, but maybe held tight to what you deserved. Worth sitting with — agency and entitlement can coexist.
reflections.internal_entitlement_mid: You steered well today, but the energy was still mostly internal. What might change if tomorrow you measured success by what you gave rather than what you received?
reflections.internal_entitlement_self: Strong on ownership, quieter on contribution. That's a legitimate place to be — but you might find more fuel in giving than in getting.
reflections.external_contribution_wide: You gave generously today and held a wide view — even while navigating a day that felt outside your control. That combination takes real steadiness.
reflections.external_contribution_mid: You contributed, even on a hard day. That's not nothing. The agency thread might feel thin right now — but it's there when you look for it.
reflections.external_contribution_self: A hard day, and you still gave something. Start there. Agency often follows contribution — not the other way around.
reflections.external_entitlement_wide: The day felt external and expectations went unmet — but you kept your gaze on something larger. That outward orientation is worth noticing and protecting.
reflections.external_entitlement_mid: Today had a heavy outer frame — things felt out of your hands, and the accounting felt uneven. That's honest. Tomorrow is a fresh ledger.
reflections.external_entitlement_self: A day that felt both constrained and unrewarded. Those days are real. If there was one moment where you had a choice — even a tiny one — that's where tomorrow might start.
